mission.title: external-secrets: Hashicorp Vault: Support Databases PostgreSQL Secrets Engine
mission.description: Hashicorp Vault: Support Databases PostgreSQL Secrets Engine. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
Support injecting dynamic secrets from the Postgres secrets engine in Hashicorp Vault: https://www.vaultproject.io/docs/secrets/databases/postgresql.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Hashicorp Vault: Support Databases PostgreSQL Secrets Engine
steps.description:
## Problem Statement
Enable Vault tokens to remain valid even after secret reading or writing is finished. This enables obtaining dynamic credentials from Vault without their leases being revoked prematurely; all the user needs to care about is ensuring that their configured Vault token TTL is
```yaml
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: SecretStore
metadata:
  name: vault-backend-db
spec:
  provider:
    vault:
      server: "http://vault.vault:8200"
      path: "database"
      version: "v1"
      auth:
        kubernetes:
          mountPath: "kubernetes"
          role: "demo-role"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Hashicorp Vault: Support Databases PostgreSQL Secrets Engine" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement
Enable Vault tokens to remain valid even after secret reading or writing is finished. This enables obtaining dynamic credentials from Vault without their leases being revoked prematurely; all the user needs to care about is ensuring that their configured Vault token TTL is longer than the dynamic secret's validity.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: SecretStore
metadata:
  name: vault-backend-db
spec:
  provider:
    vault:
      server: "http://vault.vault:8200"
      path: "database"
      version: "v1"
      auth:
        kubernetes:
          mountPath: "kubernetes"
          role: "demo-role"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
